heading: ## Falsifiability
body:
The following unified numeric thresholds replace all prior versions. Any deviation from these thresholds constitutes falsification of the central hypothesis.

| Parameter | Threshold |
|-----------|-----------|
| Primary endpoint | Proliferation rate (log2 fold change) in old-centriole vs young-centriole lineages |
| Effect size (Cohen's d) | 1.2 |
| Significance level (α) | 0.001 |
| Statistical power (1−β) | 0.95 |
| Sample size per arm | N = 43 (total N = 86 lineages) |
| Statistical test | Two-sample t-test, two-tailed |
| Secondary test (survival) | Log-rank test, α = 0.001 |
| Secondary test (correlation) | Spearman rank correlation, α = 0.001 |
| Go/no-go rule | Reject H₀ if p < 0.001 AND observed effect ≥ 0.8 d; otherwise inconclusive |

All prior falsifiability blocks (N=6, N=10, N=24; α=0.05, 0.01) are hereby superseded and must be removed.

heading: ## Pre-registration plan
body:
**OSF ID:** osf.io/TBD
**Planned registration date:** 2026-07-01

**Pre-registration content (single consolidated block):**
- Primary endpoint: proliferation rate (log2 fold change) in old-centriole vs young-centriole lineages
- Secondary endpoints: proportion of asymmetric divisions, senescence marker intensity
- Binding falsifiability thresholds: see unified table in §Falsifiability (N=43 per arm, α=0.001, power=0.95, effect size d=1.2)
- Statistical analysis plan: two-sample t-test, two-tailed, α=0.001, power=0.95
- Exclusion criteria: cell death, failed RITE labelling, technical artefacts
- Planned sample size: N=43 per arm (total N=86 lineages)
- Go/no-go decision criteria: based on unified falsifiability table only (no additional thresholds)

**Amendments:** Any changes to the above will be documented as a versioned amendment on OSF prior to data analysis.

**Linking note:** The pre-registration will contain the binding falsifiability table from §Falsifiability as an appendix. All prior pre-registration drafts (with different endpoints and stopping rules) are superseded.

heading: ## Sample size calculation
body:
The following calculation is the sole basis for the final sample size N=43 per arm. All prior calculations (N=6, N=10/15, N=24, N=34) are superseded.

**Formula:** n = (Z_α/2 + Z_β)² · 2σ² / δ²

**Parameters:**
- α = 0.001 (two-tailed) → Z_α/2 = 3.291
- Power = 0.95 → Z_β = 1.645 (for β = 0.05)
- Effect size δ = 1.2σ (standardised difference)
- σ² = 1 (variance standardised)

**Calculation:**
n = (3.291 + 1.645)² · 2 · 1 / (1.2)²
n = (4.936)² · 2 / 1.44
n = 24.36 · 2 / 1.44
n = 48.72 / 1.44
n ≈ 33.8 → rounded up to 34 per arm

**Adjustment for attrition:** Assuming 20% loss (cell death, failed labelling, technical artefacts):
N_final = 34 / 0.8 ≈ 42.5 → rounded up to 43 per arm

**Total sample size:** 86 lineages (43 old-centriole, 43 young-centriole)

**Note:** This calculation assumes equal variance between groups. If variance differs, a Welch t-test will be used with the same sample size. The effect size d=1.2 is based on pilot data (TBD) and literature precedent (Royall 2023, eLife).

heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| # | Risk | Probability (1-5) | Impact (1-5) | Mitigation |
|---|------|-------------------|--------------|------------|
| 1 | RITE labelling fails to produce distinguishable centriole ages | 3 | 5 | Pilot test on 3 cell lines; fallback: use S-phase labelling (EdU) as proxy for centriole age |
| 2 | Cell motility prevents reliable lineage tracking over 6 months | 4 | 4 | Use micro-patterned substrates (Matrigel islands) to confine cells; implement real-time drift correction |
| 3 | Algorithmic reconstruction of lineage DAG fails due to tracking errors | 3 | 4 | Manual validation of 10% of branches; use ensemble of tracking algorithms (CellTracker, TrackMate, custom CNN) |
| 4 | Centriole inheritance is not asymmetric in the target cell type | 2 | 5 | Pre-screen 5 cell types (HSC, NSC, intestinal stem cell, keratinocyte, fibroblast); select only those with ≥70% asymmetric divisions |
| 5 | Phototoxicity from long-term imaging kills cells before differentiation | 4 | 3 | Use event-driven acquisition (Mahecic 2022); limit excitation to 5% duty cycle; use spinning-disk confocal |
| 6 | Sample size insufficient to detect effect (underpowered) | 2 | 4 | Interim analysis at N=20 per arm; if effect size <0.8, increase N to 60 per arm (budget permitting) |
| 7 | Budget overrun due to extended imaging time | 3 | 3 | Reserve 15% contingency; use automated media exchange to reduce hands-on time |
| 8 | Key personnel leave before project completion | 2 | 4 | Cross-train 2 team members on each critical protocol; document SOPs in lab wiki |

heading: ## Limitations
body:
The following limitations are acknowledged. This list supersedes all prior limitations sections.

1. **Generalisability:** The study is limited to one cell type (TBD, likely HSC or NSC). Findings may not apply to other tissues with different centriole inheritance patterns.
2. **Temporal scope:** The 6-month imaging window may not capture all differentiation events, especially for slowly dividing stem cells.
3. **Technical artefacts:** RITE labelling may perturb centriole function; control experiments (wild-type centriole age markers) are planned but cannot fully exclude subtle effects.
4. **Reconstruction accuracy:** The lineage DAG is probabilistic; some branches may be incorrectly assigned despite manual validation.
5. **Sample size:** N=43 per arm is powered for d=1.2 but may miss smaller effects (d<0.8) that are biologically relevant.
6. **Causality:** The study is correlational (centriole age vs proliferation). Causal manipulation (e.g., forced centriole age reversal) is beyond scope.
7. **Scalability:** The method is labour-intensive and may not be transferable to high-throughput screening without significant automation.
